hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of National Senior Health and Fitness Day, focusing on the importance of being active and healthy, Hairspray is a fun and upbeat musical that features energetic dance numbers and a heartwarming story, making it a great fit for this observance.
hero.blurb: Hairspray is a wildly entertaining film with a talented cast, impressive choreography, and a story that promotes self-acceptance and social change. The movie's energetic atmosphere and catchy songs make it a must-watch for anyone looking for a fun and uplifting experience.

released.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End is an action-packed adventure that marked the third installment in the beloved franchise, bringing back iconic characters and introducing new ones in an epic battle to save the pirates' way of life.
released.blurb: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End is a visually stunning film with impressive special effects, memorable characters, and an engaging storyline that explores the complexities of power and loyalty. The movie's intricate plot and stunning action sequences make it a thrilling ride from start to finish.

birthday.title: The Doors
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Christopher Lambert, born May 27, 1957, who delivers a captivating performance as a fictionalized version of William S. Burroughs, a close friend of Jim Morrison, in this biographical drama that explores the life and music of the iconic rock band.
birthday.blurb: The Doors is a haunting and intense film that delves into the turbulent life of Jim Morrison, with a standout performance by Val Kilmer. The movie's atmospheric soundtrack and poetic cinematography make it a must-watch for fans of the band and anyone interested in the 1960s counterculture.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: Famously panned by critics, I Know Who Killed Me is a cringeworthy thriller with a convoluted plot, awkward dialogue, and a general air of confusion, making it a perfect candidate for a dumpster dive.
awful.blurb: I Know Who Killed Me is a laughably bad movie that tries to pass off its incoherent storyline as a clever twist, but ends up being a mess of cliches and poor acting. The film's inadvertent humor and over-the-top performances make it a so-bad-it's-good watch for those who enjoy trainwrecks.
